category_title: High-proficiency international students with advanced literary skills and linguistic perfectionism
minimal_context: The student is writing a 300-word blog reflection on their Personal Narrative assignment using the class's online blog platform.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please begin writing your 300-word blog reflection on the Personal Narrative assignment.

STUDENT: I am starting, but I want to try to play around with words, crafting bombastic and colorful sentences for my opening.

TUTOR: Okay, keep working on it.

STUDENT: I actually do spend a lofty amount of time deciding how to phrase my sentences. I am tentative to how my sentences would read out on the blog.

TUTOR: Just write what you think.

STUDENT: I had rephrased the same line, over and over again, out of frustration, trying to rectify how awkward it would sound. How much of these could I use before crossing the line of sounding like nonsensical ramblings?

options.A: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the blog reflection assignments and daily writing instruction.
options.B: Implement a three-revision cycle for the blog reflection where only the final, third draft is graded.
options.C: Implement continuous systematic assessment, frequently requiring the student to show daily blog progress for an immediate grade.
options.D: Publicly celebrate the student's writing milestones and drafting improvements on the blog platform as often as possible.
